## Overview
### Introduction to Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B
Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B is a standard, non-open-source model provided by Qwen, released on January 1, 2024. This model boasts an impressive architecture, with a context window of 262,144 tokens and a maximum output of 65,536 tokens. The knowledge cutoff for this model is December 2023, ensuring it is trained on a vast amount of data up to that point. The pricing for Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B is $0.26 per 1M tokens for input and $2.08 per 1M tokens for output.

### Architecture and Strengths
The Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B model has several key strengths, including its capabilities in text, function calling, JSON mode, streaming, and structured outputs. It is best suited for applications such as chat, text generation, coding, analysis, RAG pipelines, and summarization. The model's performance is backed by its benchmark scores, including an MMLU score of 87.0 and an LMSYS Arena ELO score of 1270. With its robust architecture and capabilities, Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B is a powerful tool for developers looking to integrate advanced language processing into their applications.

### Use Cases and Cost Considerations
Developers can leverage Qwen: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B for a variety of use cases, including chatbots, text generation, and coding applications. The cost of using this model is relatively affordable, with an estimated $0.0012 for 1,000 calls (avg 500 tokens), $0.011999999999999999 for 10,000 calls, and $0.12 for 100,000 calls. With no direct
